title: leichhardtia quadrata
intro: Leichhardtia quadrata, also known as the tree stinkwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its roots and leaves are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Leichhardtia quadrata include wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often made into a tea or infused in oil to utilize its medicinal properties.
